report_name: 23andMe Reaches $18M Bankruptcy Settlement Over 2023 Data Breach

attack_patterns.name: Credential Stuffing
attack_patterns.description: Attackers used lists of usernames and passwords stolen from other data breaches to systematically try them against 23andMe accounts, leading to the compromise of nearly 7 million customer accounts.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: Attackers gained initial access to 23andMe customer accounts by using legitimate credentials that were successfully validated through credential stuffing attacks.

identities.name: 23andMe
identities.description: A genetic testing company that experienced a data breach in October 2023 due to credential stuffing attacks, exposing the data of nearly 7 million customers. The company's security failures, such as a lack of mandatory MFA, led to an $18 million bankruptcy settlement.

identities.name: 23andMe Customers
identities.description: A class of nearly 7 million customers, including almost 200,000 in Pennsylvania, whose sensitive genetic and personal data was exposed in the 2023 data breach of 23andMe.

indicators.name: Web Authentication Failure Indicative of Credential Stuffing
indicators.pattern:
title: Web Authentication Failure
id: 2b9a7c3d-f1e4-4b8a-9c6d-0e8f1a2b3c4d
status: experimental
description: Detects a single web authentication failure (e.g., HTTP 401/403). This rule is intended to be used in a SIEM to build a correlation that triggers on a high number of these events from a single source IP or against a single user account, which is indicative of credential stuffing or brute force attacks as seen in the 23andMe breach.
references:
  - https://levittownnow.com/2026/07/17/pa-settles-after-23andme-data-breach/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-18
tags:
  - attack.t1110.003
  - attack.credential_access
level: informational
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    sc_status:
      - 401
      - 403
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects a single web authentication failure (e.g., HTTP 401/403). This rule is intended to be used in a SIEM to build a correlation that triggers on a high number of these events from a single source IP or against a single user account, which is indicative of credential stuffing or brute force attacks as seen in the 23andMe breach.

relationships.source_name: Web Authentication Failure Indicative of Credential Stuffing
